data.title: Tillis backs Warsh for Fed Chair after DOJ drops Powell probe
data.description:
Republican Senator Thom Tillis said Sunday he will allow Kevin Warsh’s nomination for Federal Reserve chair to move forward after the Department of Justice ended its investigation into current Fed Chair Jerome Powell. Tillis had previously vowed to block any nominee while the probe remained open.

The development is important to investors because it reduces uncertainty around Fed leadership at a time when markets are focused on interest rates, inflation and economic growth. A smoother transition at the central bank could calm concerns about political interference and help markets focus on future monetary policy decisions.

"I am prepared to move on with the confirmation of Mr. Warsh," Tillis said on NBC’s Meet the Press. "I think he's going to be a great Fed chair."

The decision likely clears a path for Warsh to win Senate confirmation before May 15, when Powell’s term as chair expires. Republicans on the Senate Banking Committee now appear to have enough votes to advance the nomination despite unified Democratic opposition.

Powell disclosed in January that the DOJ had opened a criminal investigation into his handling of a $2.5 billion renovation of two Fed buildings in Washington. He called the probe intimidation and part of broader efforts to pressure the Fed to cut interest rates. A federal judge later blocked subpoenas tied to the case.

On Friday, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said the DOJ would end the investigation and instead refer the matter to the Fed’s inspector general, which was already reviewing the project.

Tillis said his concerns were never about Warsh, whose qualifications he described during confirmation hearings as “impeccable.” Warsh, a former Fed governor, has pledged to review the Fed’s policy framework and coordinate more closely with other parts of government on non-monetary issues.

Powell has said he would temporarily remain chair if Warsh is not confirmed by May 15. He could also stay on as a Fed governor beyond that date under his existing term.

"I have no intention of leaving the Board until the investigation is well and truly over, ⁠with transparency and finality," Powell said last month.

data.title: Quant snapshot: AXT, Chevron lead top-rated names as CBIZ, Grid Dynamics lag
data.description:
Quant rankings for the upcoming earnings calendar show strength concentrated across information technology, energy, and materials, while weakness is more pronounced in industrials, financials, and real estate. A total of 966 companies are scheduled to report in the coming week.

This week’s top-rated names are led by AXT (AXTI), which holds a quant rating of 4.98, followed by Chevron Corporation (CVX) and Darling Ingredients (DAR), both at 4.95. Dana Incorporated (DAN) and BNP Paribas SA (BNPQY) also feature prominently with ratings of 4.94. Materials names Constellium SE (CSTM) and Centerra Gold (CGAU) rank among the leaders, alongside healthcare name Astellas Pharma (ALPMY) and technology player Celestica (CLS), reflecting broadly strong factor readings across valuation, growth, and profitability.

On the downside, industrials, financials, and real estate dominate the weakest-ranked stocks. CBIZ (CBZ) holds the lowest quant score at 1.21, followed by Grid Dynamics Holdings (GDYN) at 1.23 and GBank Financial Holdings (GBFH) at 1.30. Other laggards include Alkami Technology (ALKT), Empire State Realty Trust (ESRT), and Casella Waste Systems (CWST), alongside Camping World Holdings (CWH), Herc Holdings (HRI), Avantor (AVTR), and Fiverr International (FVRR), pointing to weaker factor trends across valuation and momentum.

Other high-profile companies set to report earnings this week include Apple (AAPL), Microsoft Corporation (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOG), and Meta Platforms (META), all of which carry Hold ratings with quant scores of 3.48, 3.48, 3.45, 3.48, and 3.41, respectively.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system grades stocks based on their relative performance on critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are assigned on a scale from 1 to 5, with any score of 3.5 or above considered a bullish rating and any score of 2.5 or below indicating a bearish assessment.

Here are the top-rated upcoming earnings stocks, ranked by Quant:

  • AXT (AXTI), Quant Rating: 4.98.
  • Chevron Corporation (CVX), Quant Rating: 4.95.
  • Darling Ingredients (DAR), Quant Rating: 4.95.
  • Dana Incorporated (DAN), Quant Rating: 4.94.
  • BNP Paribas SA (BNPQY), Quant Rating: 4.94.
  • Constellium SE (CSTM), Quant Rating: 4.93.
  • Astellas Pharma (ALPMY), Quant Rating: 4.89.
  • Celestica (CLS), Quant Rating: 4.88.
  • Centerra Gold (CGAU), Quant Rating: 4.88.
  • Sendas Distribuidora (ASAIY), Quant Rating: 4.87.

Here are the bottom-rated upcoming earnings stocks, ranked by Quant:

  • CBIZ (CBZ), Quant Rating: 1.21.
  • Grid Dynamics Holdings (GDYN), Quant Rating: 1.23.
  • GBank Financial Holdings (GBFH), Quant Rating: 1.30.
  • Alkami Technology (ALKT), Quant Rating: 1.32.
  • Empire State Realty Trust (ESRT), Quant Rating: 1.36.
  • Casella Waste Systems (CWST), Quant Rating: 1.36.
  • Camping World Holdings (CWH), Quant Rating: 1.36.
  • Herc Holdings (HRI), Quant Rating: 1.40.
  • Avantor (AVTR), Quant Rating: 1.42.
  • Fiverr International (FVRR), Quant Rating: 1.43.

data.title: White House dinner shooting suspect identified as Caltech graduate, 'teacher of the month'
data.description:
Authorities identified the suspect in Saturday night’s shooting near the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner as 31-year-old Cole Allen of Torrance, California, according to multiple press reports. Two law-enforcement officials said Allen allegedly opened fire near the Washington Hilton, where President Trump was attending the event.

Police said Allen was carrying a shotgun, handgun and knives and had been staying as a guest at the hotel. One law-enforcement officer was wounded during the attack. Investigators said they believe Allen acted alone, though no motive or intended target has been disclosed.

President Trump described the suspect as a “lone wolf” during remarks after the incident.

Allen graduated from the California Institute of Technology in 2017 with a degree in mechanical engineering, according to school records. He later earned a master’s degree in computer science from California State University, Dominguez Hills, graduating in 2025.

Former acquaintances expressed shock after learning of the arrest. One former Caltech associate said the news was difficult to comprehend.

Allen also worked for C2 Education, a tutoring and test-preparation company, where he was recognized as “teacher of the month” in December 2024, according to a company social media post.

Public voting records listed him as having no party affiliation. Federal Election Commission filings show he made a $25 donation through ActBlue in 2024 earmarked for the Harris presidential campaign.

Allen lives in Torrance, a Los Angeles County suburb. He is expected to appear in court Monday and faces charges including use of a firearm during a violent crime and assault on federal officers with a dangerous weapon. Authorities said he was taken to a hospital for evaluation Saturday night.

data.title: Near-term odds of U.S.-Iran peace deal dip after a U-turn on Pakistan trip
data.description:
Odds of a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran before the end of May dipped after U.S. envoys were not deployed to Pakistan for talks this weekend.

Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner did not travel to Pakistan for planned talks with Iranian counterparts. President Donald Trump said Saturday that Iran “can call us anytime they want.”

Odds of a deal on Polymarket fell to 25% from 35% following the report, before stabilizing around 30%. Odds of a deal before the end of April stand at 2%, while odds of a deal before the end of June rose to 50% before easing back to 47%.

data.title: Iran conflict sends pistachio prices soaring as global supply tightens
data.description:
The war in Iran has driven pistachio prices to their highest levels in years, disrupting exports just as global demand for pistachio-based foods continues to surge, The Financial Times reported Sunday.

Iran is one of the world’s largest pistachio producers, accounting for roughly one-fifth of global output and up to 30% of exports in some years. But fighting in the region has complicated shipping routes, increased freight costs and slowed trade flows across the Middle East.

Benchmark pistachio prices climbed to about $4.57 a pound in March, the highest since 2018. Demand has been boosted by the popularity of pistachio products, including Dubai chocolate bars filled with pistachio cream, which went viral online and helped spark a broader flavor trend.

Supply was already under strain before the conflict. Harvests in 2025 fell short in major producing countries including the U.S., Turkey and Iran, where drought damaged crops. Sanctions, domestic unrest and internet shutdowns had also made it harder for Iranian exporters to coordinate with overseas buyers.

The war has added further pressure. Traders said shipping through Bandar Abbas, a key Iranian port near the Strait of Hormuz, has been heavily disrupted. Some exports continue through Turkey or rail routes to China, but those options are slower and more expensive. Iranian customs data showed exports dropped 30% over the past two months from a year earlier.

Buyers are increasingly turning to alternative suppliers such as the U.S., which produces about 40% of the world’s pistachios. But available U.S. supplies are already largely sold.

Importers say Iranian pistachios remain difficult to replace because of their higher oil content, which gives them a richer taste and better performance in baked goods.

Traders warn that if Iranian shipments remain constrained, pistachio prices could continue climbing.

data.title: Notable healthcare headlines for the week: UnitedHealth, Eli Lilly, and Organon in focus
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages ended mixed on Friday, but the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq achieved another record close as investors digested the latest developments in the Middle East, including a three-week extension to the truce between Israel and Lebanon.

The benchmark S&P 500 ended +0.8%, reaching the all-time high close of 7,165.08 points, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq Composite closed +1.6%, also at the record close of 24,836.60 points.

Meanwhile, the blue-chip Dow was down 0.2%.

Week-to-date, the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq ended +0.5% and +1.5%, respectively, while the Dow finished -0.4%.

The S&P 500 Health Care Index Sector (XLV)  slipped above 3% during the week.

The top S&P 500 healthcare gainers and losers for the last week are as follows:

Top Gainers:

West Pharmaceutical Services (WST) +11.85%

Centene (CNC) +9.56%

UnitedHealth (UNH) +9.33%

 Elevance Health (ELV) +6.72%

Humana (HUM) +4.92%

Top Losers:

HCA Healthcare (HCA) -11.38%

Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) -10.80%

Danaher (DHR) -8.99%

Bio-Techne (TECH) -8.51%

The Cooper Companies (COO) -8.05%

Here are some of the important healthcare stories from this week:

UnitedHealth lifts managed care peers after Q1 beat, guidance raise

Shares of managed care organizations rose on Tuesday after UnitedHealth (UNH), the industry bellwether for the earnings season, reported better-than-expected Q1 2026 results and increased its full-year earnings outlook.

While UnitedHealth (UNH) added ~7%, its peers, including Humana (HUM), CVS Health (CVS), Cigna (CI), Clover Health (CLOV), and Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), also traded higher.

Minnesota-based UnitedHealth (UNH) reported $111.7B in revenue for the quarter with ~2% YoY growth, exceeding the consensus by $2.1B, thanks mainly to its insurance segment. Meanwhile, its non-GAAP earnings per share reached $7.23, largely unchanged from Q4 2025 but beating Street forecasts by $0.63.

In conjunction with the financial results, UnitedHealth (UNH) announced plans to repurchase up to $2.0B in its common stock by the end of Q2 2026.

UnitedHealth (UNH) lifted its full-year earnings outlook to more than $18.25 per share on a non-GAAP basis compared to $17.87 in the consensus and more than $17.75 in the prior forecast.

Organon climbs amid reports of $13B buyout bid from Sun Pharma

Organon (OGN) shares jumped 31% on Friday to $11.26 after media reports indicated that India’s Sun Pharmaceutical Industries is moving ahead with its biggest-ever M&A deal by submitting a $13B binding offer for the U.S. drugmaker.

Mumbai-based Sun Pharma is competing with German drugmaker Grünenthal and Swedish private equity firm EQT for a potential deal, according to the story first reported by The Economic Times newspaper in India and later picked up by other local outlets.

Shares of the Merck (MRK) spinoff have lost ~12% this week, indicating a market capitalization of roughly $2.2B for the company, which had approximately $8.6B in total long-term debt as of Dec. 31.

The stock closed ~28% higher on April 10, after The Economic Times reported that Sun Pharma had submitted a binding offer worth $12B to acquire Organon (OGN) in an all-cash transaction.

Lilly to acquire cancer drug developer Kelonia for up to $7B

Eli Lilly (LLY) on Monday announced an agreement to acquire Kelonia Therapeutics, a privately held cancer drug developer, in a deal worth up to $7B in cash, confirming a prior report from The Wall Street Journal.

The transaction expected to close in H2 2026 will provide the weight loss drugmaker with Kelonia's lead asset, KLN-1010, a one-time CAR-T cell therapy designed to target a blood cancer known as multiple myeloma.

As part of the agreement, the Indiana-based pharma giant is set to pay up to $7B to Kelonia shareholders, including $3.25B upfront and additional payments that will depend on clinical, regulatory, and commercial milestones.

Eli Lilly (LLY) added that it has yet to determine the GAAP-based accounting treatment for the buyout, which it said will be reflected in its upcoming financial results and guidance.

In other news of note, obesity drugmakers Novo Nordisk (NVO) and Eli Lilly (LLY) traded lower on Tuesday after Bloomberg News reported that leading health insurers remain undecided on whether to join the Trump administration's plans to cover weight loss drugs for Medicare beneficiaries next year.

Bayer faces high-stakes Roundup test as plaintiffs weigh $7.5B settlement

Bayer (BAYZF) (BAYRY) is confronting a pivotal moment in its long-running Roundup litigation as thousands of plaintiffs decide whether to accept a proposed $7.5 billion settlement or continue pursuing individual lawsuits, a choice that could shape the company’s future legal exposure, The Wall Street Journal reported Monday.

The German pharmaceutical and agriculture group has spent years trying to contain claims that Roundup weedkiller caused cancer, allegations Bayer denies. The company says the product is safe and supported by regulatory reviews. A Missouri judge gave preliminary approval in March to the latest settlement framework, which would raise Bayer’s (BAYZF) (BAYRY) total Roundup-related costs to roughly $22 billion.

For investors, the outcome is significant because Bayer (BAYZF) (BAYRY) needs broad participation in the settlement to reduce uncertainty that has weighed on its valuation since the 2018 Monsanto acquisition. If too many claimants opt out, Bayer (BAYZF) (BAYRY) may remain exposed to years of costly litigation, and management has already said it could consider other options, including restructuring measures such as bankruptcy-related strategies.

Boston Scientific cuts full-year outlook despite Q1 beat

Boston Scientific (BSX) on Wednesday lowered its full-year outlook despite reporting better-than-expected financials for Q1 2026.

The company projects its 2026 revenue to grow at roughly 7.0% YoY to 8.5% YoY on a reported basis compared to 10.5% to 11.5% estimated with its Q4 2025 financials in February and ~11% YoY growth implied in the consensus.

Its non-GAAP earnings per share for the year are expected to reach $3.34 - $3.41 compared to $3.43 - $3.49 previously and $3.45 in the consensus.

As for the current quarter, the Marlborough, Massachusetts-based firm expects approximately 5.5% YoY–7.5% YoY growth and $0.82–$0.84 in adjusted EPS compared to ~10% YoY growth and $0.86 in the consensus, respectively.

However, for Q1, the company did beat Street forecasts, reporting $5.2B in revenue with ~12% YoY growth and $0.80 of adjusted EPS, exceeding the consensus by $30M and $0.01, respectively.

Merck inks AI deal worth up to $1B with Google Cloud

Merck (MRK) and Google Cloud (GOOG) announced a partnership worth up to $1B on Wednesday to implement an agentic platform across the drugmaker's business operations, making it an AI-driven enterprise.

As part of the multi-year deal, the companies are set to deploy Google Cloud's offerings, including Gemini Enterprise, to digitize data and help boost the productivity of Merck's (MRK) more than 75K-strong workforce.
